goal: The repo's migrations match the live DB, and the seams are protected by tests.
why: Phases 1 and 2 added auth and encryption to a codebase with zero tests and a scheduled_emails table that exists in the live DB but not on disk. This phase makes the repo reproducible (tracked + reconciled migrations) and the seams refactor-safe (vitest seam/route/util tests).
success_criteria: supabase/migrations/0007_scheduled_emails.sql exists with all 11 columns, the four-value status check, and RLS-on/no-policy; npx supabase db diff is clean (or schema-match self-check passes if no local stack).
mail_accounts has exactly one authoritative email-uniqueness rule; the redundant lower(email) expression index is dropped by 0008.
vitest is a devDependency and npm test runs the suite green.
Seam tests pass for Graph, OneDrive ops, IMAP read, SMTP send, and runDue() per-row isolation with vendors mocked.
The Phase-2 encryption round-trip and Phase-1 session sign/verify tests pass against the real utils.
The agent tool-dispatch test passes (success, validation-error, unknown-tool no-throw).

tasks.title: Track the scheduled_emails migration to match the live table
acceptance_criteria: 0007_scheduled_emails.sql creates public.scheduled_emails with every column scheduled.ts reads/writes (id, from_email, to_email, subject, body, scheduled_at, status, sent_at, error, created_by, created_at).
A check constraint restricts status to exactly pending/sent/failed/cancelled.
RLS enabled with no policies (service-role-only lockdown matching 0003).
Migration is idempotent (create table if not exists) and self-contained.
npx supabase db diff is clean for scheduled_emails, or a schema-match self-check passes if no local stack.
tasks.action: Reconstruct columns from ScheduledRow (scheduled.ts:29-41). Create public.scheduled_emails (if not exists): id uuid pk; from_email/to_email/subject/body text not null; scheduled_at timestamptz not null; status text default pending; sent_at timestamptz; error text; created_by text nullable; created_at default now(). Add a check on the four status values, an index on (status, scheduled_at), RLS enabled no policies. Verify via supabase db diff or the grep self-check.

tasks.title: Reconcile mail_accounts to one authoritative email-uniqueness constraint
acceptance_criteria: 0008 drops the redundant lower(email) expression index (mail_accounts_email_unique from 0003:25) and keeps the plain unique(email) (mail_accounts_email_key from 0005:6) as the single authoritative rule.
Case-insensitive correctness is preserved by application-layer lowercasing (accounts.ts:85, scheduled.ts:86-87) documented in the migration comment.
Migration is idempotent (drop index if exists) and runs cleanly regardless of prior migration state.
tasks.action: Confirm both constraints (0003:25 expression index, 0005:5-6 plain constraint). In 0008 run drop index if exists public.mail_accounts_email_unique. Comment that mail_accounts_email_key (plain unique email) is authoritative, the index is removed, and case-insensitivity holds via app-layer lowercasing (accounts.ts:85, scheduled.ts:86-87). Keep mail_accounts_email_key; add no new constraint.

tasks.title: Add vitest + seam/route test safety net
acceptance_criteria: npm test runs vitest and exits 0; package.json has a test: vitest run script and vitest in devDependencies.
npx tsc --noEmit stays clean; the @/ alias resolves in both tsc and vitest.

Seam tests pass for Graph transport (auth header + GraphError), OneDrive ops (GraphDriveItem to DriveItem mapping), IMAP read (folder resolution + envelope formatting), SMTP send (from header + transport.sendMail), and runDue() per-row isolation (one failure still flips others to sent).
Phase-2 encryption round-trip passes: decrypt(encrypt(x))===x and ciphertext differs from plaintext, against the real util discovered at build time.
Phase-1 session sign/verify passes: verify(sign(principal))===principal and an invalid token is rejected, against the real util discovered at build time.
Agent tool-dispatch passes: send_email success JSON, send_email validation-error JSON, unknown-tool returns a readable string and does not throw.
No test hits a real Graph/IMAP/SMTP/Supabase endpoint — all vendors mocked.
tasks.action: STEP 1: grep src for the real session and encrypt/decrypt util paths (deviation if not session.ts/secrets.ts). STEP 2: add vitest devDep + test script. STEP 3: vitest.config.ts node env, globals, @/ alias, setupFiles. STEP 4: vitest.setup.ts stubs env. STEP 5: seam tests via vi.mock (fetch+connections, graph, imapflow+mailparser+accounts, nodemailer, supabase+accounts+smtp, tool deps). STEP 6/7: encryption round-trip + session sign/verify vs real utils. STEP 8: npm test and tsc clean.
